    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# Erd≈ës Problem 612
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/612](https://www.erdosproblems.com/612)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $G$ be a connected graph with $n$ vertices, minimum degree $d$, and diameter $D$.
    25‚Üí
    26‚ÜíThe original conjecture of Erd≈ës, Pach, Pollack, and Tuza [EPPT89] stated:
    27‚Üí- If $G$ contains no $K_{2r}$ and $(r-1)(3r+2) \mid d$ then
    28‚Üí  $D \leq \frac{2(r-1)(3r+2)}{2r^2-1} \cdot \frac{n}{d} + O(1)$
    29‚Üí- If $G$ contains no $K_{2r+1}$ and $(3r-1) \mid d$ then
    30‚Üí  $D \leq \frac{3r-1}{r} \cdot \frac{n}{d} + O(1)$
    31‚Üí
    32‚ÜíThe first case was disproven for $r \geq 2$ by Czabarka, Singgih, and Sz√©kely [CSS21].
    33‚Üí
    34‚ÜíThe amended conjecture (due to [CSS21]) states that if $G$ contains no $K_{k+1}$ then
    35‚Üí$D \leq (3 - 2/k) \cdot n/d + O(1)$.
    36‚Üí
    37‚ÜíThis is known:
    38‚Üí- For $k = 2$ ($K_3$-free), proved in [EPPT89]
    39‚Üí- For $3$-colourable graphs (weaker than $K_4$-free), by Czabarka, Dankelmann,
    40‚Üí  Sz√©kely [CDS09]
    41‚Üí- For $4$-colourable graphs (weaker than $K_5$-free), by Czabarka, Smith,
    42‚Üí  Sz√©kely [CSS23]
    43‚Üí
    44‚ÜíIt is also known that any connected graph on $n$ vertices with minimum degree $d$ has
    45‚Üí$D \leq 3n/(d+1) + O(1)$.
    46‚Üí
    47‚Üí[EPPT89] Erd≈ës, P., Pach, J., Pollack, R., and Tuza, Zs., _Radius, diameter, and
    48‚Üíminimum degree_. J. Combin. Theory Ser. B 47 (1989), 73-79.
    49‚Üí
    50‚Üí[CSS21] Czabarka, √â., Singgih, O., and Sz√©kely, L.A., _Counterexample to a conjecture
    51‚Üíof Erd≈ës, Pach, Pollack, and Tuza on diameter and minimum degree_. 2021.
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üí[CDS09] Czabarka, √â., Dankelmann, P., and Sz√©kely, L.A., _Diameter and degree
    54‚Üíconditions for $K_{t,t}$-free graphs_. 2009.
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí[CSS23] Czabarka, √â., Smith, S.J., and Sz√©kely, L.A., _Diameter bounds for
    57‚Üí4-colourable graphs_. 2023.
    58‚Üí-/

    60‚Üíopen SimpleGraph
    61‚Üí
    62‚Üínamespace Erdos612
    63‚Üí
    64‚Üí/--
    65‚Üí**Erd≈ës Problem 612** (Amended conjecture, [CSS21]):
    66‚Üí
    67‚ÜíIf $G$ is a connected $K_{k+1}$-free graph on $n$ vertices with minimum degree $d \geq 1$,
    68‚Üíthen
    69‚Üí$$\operatorname{diam}(G) \leq \left(3 - \frac{2}{k}\right) \cdot \frac{n}{d} + C$$
    70‚Üífor some constant $C$ depending only on $k$.
    71‚Üí-/
    72‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5]
    73‚Üítheorem erdos_612 (k : ‚Ñï) (hk : k ‚â• 2) :
    74‚Üí    ‚àÉ C : ‚Ñù, ‚àÄ (n : ‚Ñï) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) [DecidableRel G.Adj],
    75‚Üí      G.Connected ‚Üí
    76‚Üí      G.CliqueFree (k + 1) ‚Üí
    77‚Üí      G.minDegree ‚â• 1 ‚Üí
    78‚Üí      (G.diam : ‚Ñù) ‚â§ (3 - 2 / (k : ‚Ñù)) * ((n : ‚Ñù) / (G.minDegree : ‚Ñù)) + C := by
    79‚Üí  sorry
    80‚Üí
    81‚Üí/--
    82‚Üí**Erd≈ës Problem 612** (Known case $k = 2$, [EPPT89]):
    83‚Üí
    84‚ÜíIf $G$ is a connected triangle-free graph on $n$ vertices with minimum degree $d \geq 1$,
    85‚Üíthen
    86‚Üí$$\operatorname{diam}(G) \leq 2 \cdot \frac{n}{d} + C$$
    87‚Üífor some absolute constant $C$.
    88‚Üí-/
    89‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    90‚Üítheorem erdos_612.variants.triangle_free :
    91‚Üí    ‚àÉ C : ‚Ñù, ‚àÄ (n : ‚Ñï) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) [DecidableRel G.Adj],
    92‚Üí      G.Connected ‚Üí
    93‚Üí      G.CliqueFree 3 ‚Üí
    94‚Üí      G.minDegree ‚â• 1 ‚Üí
    95‚Üí      (G.diam : ‚Ñù) ‚â§ 2 * ((n : ‚Ñù) / (G.minDegree : ‚Ñù)) + C := by
    96‚Üí  sorry
    97‚Üí
    98‚Üí/--
    99‚ÜíKnown general bound ([EPPT89]):
   100‚Üí

   106‚Üí    ‚àÉ C : ‚Ñù, ‚àÄ (n : ‚Ñï) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) [DecidableRel G.Adj],
   107‚Üí      G.Connected ‚Üí

   110‚Üí  sorry

   112‚Üíend Erdos612

   132‚Üí
   133‚Üí/-- The chromatic cardinal is the minimal number of colors need to color it. In contrast to
   134‚Üí`chromaticNumber`, which assigns `‚ä§ : ‚Ñï‚àû` to all non-finitely colorable graphs, this definition
   135‚Üíreturns a `Cardinal` and can therefore distinguish between different infinite chromatic numbers. -/
   136‚Üínoncomputable def chromaticCardinal.{u} {V : Type u} (G : SimpleGraph V) : Cardinal :=
   137‚Üí  sInf {Œ∫ : Cardinal | ‚àÉ (C : Type u) (_ : Cardinal.mk C = Œ∫), Nonempty (G.Coloring C)}
   138‚Üí
   139‚Üí/-- The maximum size of the union of k finite independent sets. -/
   140‚Üínoncomputable def indepNumK (G : SimpleGraph V) (k : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï :=
   141‚Üí  sSup {n | ‚àÉ f : Fin k ‚Üí Set V, (‚àÄ i, G.IsIndepSet (f i)) ‚àß (‚ãÉ i, f i).ncard = n}
   142‚Üí
   143‚Üí/-- A finite graph is CDS-colorable if it has a proper coloring
   144‚Üíby natural numbers such that for all `k > 0`, the number of
   145‚Üívertices with color `< k` equals the maximum size of
   146‚Üíthe union of `k` independent sets. -/
   147‚Üídef CDSColorable [Fintype Œ±] {G : SimpleGraph Œ±} : Prop :=
   148‚Üí    ‚àÉ (C : G.Coloring Nat), ‚àÄ k : Nat,
   149‚Üí   ‚àë i < k, (C.colorClass i).ncard = indepNumK G k
   150‚Üí
   151‚Üí/-- A homomorphism is rainbow if it maps distinct edges to distinct colors. -/
   152‚Üídef IsRainbow {Œ± V : Type*} {H : SimpleGraph Œ±} {G : SimpleGraph V} (f : H ‚Üíg G) {C : Type*}
   153‚Üí    (c : Sym2 V ‚Üí C) : Prop :=
   154‚Üí  Function.Injective fun e : H.edgeSet => c (Sym2.map f e)
   155‚Üí
   156‚Üí/--
   157‚ÜíThe anti-Ramsey number $\mathrm{AR}(n, H)$: maximum colors to edge-color $K_n$ without rainbow $H$.
   158‚Üí-/
   159‚Üínoncomputable def antiRamseyNum {Œ± : Type*} [Fintype Œ±] (H : SimpleGraph Œ±) (n : ‚Ñï) : ‚Ñï :=
   160‚Üí  sSup {k | ‚àÉ c : Sym2 (Fin n) ‚Üí Fin k, ‚àÄ f : H ‚Üíg ‚ä§, ¬¨IsRainbow f c}
   161‚Üí

1‚Üí/-

    15‚Üí-/
    16‚Üíimport FormalConjecturesForMathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.GraphConjectures.Definitions
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjecturesForMathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.GraphConjectures.Domination
    18‚Üíimport Mathlib.Analysis.Matrix.Spectrum
    19‚Üíimport Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.AdjMatrix
    20‚Üíimport Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Metric
    21‚Üíimport Mathlib.Data.Multiset.Interval
    22‚Üí
    23‚Üínoncomputable def Matrix.IsHermitian.maxEigenvalue {ùïú : Type*} [Field ùïú] [RCLike ùïú]
    24‚Üí    {n : Type*} [Fintype n] [DecidableEq n] {A : Matrix n n ùïú} (hA : A.IsHermitian) : ‚Ñù :=
    25‚Üí  iSup hA.eigenvalues
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üínamespace SimpleGraph
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üíopen Classical
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üívariable {Œ± : Type*} [Fintype Œ±] [DecidableEq Œ±]
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üí/-- Abbreviation for the average independence number of the neighborhoods.
    34‚Üí-/
    35‚Üínoncomputable abbrev l (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñù := averageIndepNeighbors G
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí/-- The same quantity under a different name, used in some conjectures.
    38‚Üí-/
    39‚Üínoncomputable abbrev l_avg (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñù := averageIndepNeighbors G
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí/-- Independent domination number of `G`. -/
    42‚Üínoncomputable def gi (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñï := G.indepDominationNumber
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí/-- `temp_v G v = deg(v)/(n(G) - deg(v))`. -/
    45‚Üínoncomputable def temp_v (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] (v : Œ±) : ‚Ñù :=
    46‚Üí  let n := Fintype.card Œ±
    47‚Üí  let deg := G.degree v
    48‚Üí  if n = deg then 0 else (deg : ‚Ñù) / ((n : ‚Ñù) - (deg : ‚Ñù))
    49‚Üí
    50‚Üí/-- Maximum of `temp_v` over all vertices. -/
    51‚Üínoncomputable def MaxTemp (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] [Fintype Œ±] [Nonempty Œ±] : ‚Ñù :=
    52‚Üí  let temps := Finset.univ.image (temp_v G)
    53‚Üí  temps.max' (Finset.image_nonempty.mpr Finset.univ_nonempty)
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí/-- Cardinality of the union of the neighbourhoods of the ends of the non-edge `e`. -/
    56‚Üídef non_edge_neighborhood_card (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] (e : Sym2 Œ±) : ‚Ñï :=
    57‚Üí  Sym2.lift ‚ü®fun u v => (G.neighborFinset u ‚à™ G.neighborFinset v).card,
    58‚Üí    fun u v => by simp [Finset.union_comm]‚ü© e
    59‚Üí
    60‚Üí/-- Minimum size of the neighbourhood of a non-edge of `G`. -/
    61‚Üínoncomputable def NG (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ñù :=
    62‚Üí  let non_edges := (compl G).edgeFinset
    63‚Üí  if h : non_edges.Nonempty then
    64‚Üí    let neighbor_sizes := non_edges.image (non_edge_neighborhood_card G)
    65‚Üí    (neighbor_sizes.min' (Finset.Nonempty.image h _))
    66‚Üí  else
    67‚Üí    (Fintype.card Œ± : ‚Ñù)
    68‚Üí
    69‚Üí/-- Size of a largest induced forest of `G` expressed as a real number. -/
    70‚Üínoncomputable def S (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñù :=
    71‚Üí  let card := Fintype.card Œ±
    72‚Üí  if card < 2 then 0 else
    73‚Üí    let degrees := Multiset.ofList (List.map (fun v => G.degree v) Finset.univ.toList)
    74‚Üí    let sorted_degrees := degrees.sort (¬∑ ‚â§ ¬∑)
    75‚Üí    ‚Üë((sorted_degrees[card - 2]?).getD 0)
    76‚Üí
    77‚Üí/-- Local eccentricity of a vertex. -/
    78‚Üínoncomputable def local_eccentricity (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) (v : Œ±) : ENat :=
    79‚Üí  sSup (Set.range (G.edist v))
    80‚Üí
    81‚Üí/-- The largest integer less than or equal to `x`. -/
    82‚Üínoncomputable def FLOOR (x : ‚Ñù) : ‚Ñù := ‚åäx‚åã
    83‚Üí
    84‚Üí/-- Eccentricity of a vertex. -/
    85‚Üínoncomputable def eccentricity (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) (v : Œ±) : ‚Ñï‚àû :=
    86‚Üí  sSup (Set.range (G.edist v))
    87‚Üí
    88‚Üí/-- The minimum eccentricity among all vertices. -/
    89‚Üínoncomputable def minEccentricity (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñï‚àû :=
    90‚Üí  sInf (Set.range (eccentricity G))
    91‚Üí
    92‚Üí/-- The set of vertices of minimum eccentricity. -/
    93‚Üínoncomputable def graphCenter (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : Set Œ± :=
    94‚Üí  {v : Œ± | eccentricity G v = minEccentricity G}
    95‚Üí
    96‚Üí/-- The maximum eccentricity among all vertices. -/
    97‚Üínoncomputable def maxEccentricity (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñï‚àû :=
    98‚Üí  sSup (Set.range (eccentricity G))
    99‚Üí
   100‚Üí/-- The set of vertices of maximum eccentricity. -/
   101‚Üínoncomputable def maxEccentricityVertices (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : Set Œ± :=
   102‚Üí  {v : Œ± | eccentricity G v = maxEccentricity G}
   103‚Üí
   104‚Üí/-- Distance from a vertex to a finite set. -/
   105‚Üínoncomputable def distToSet (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) (v : Œ±) (S : Set Œ±) : ‚Ñï :=
   106‚Üí  if h : S.toFinset.Nonempty then
   107‚Üí    (S.toFinset.image (fun s => G.dist v s)).min' (Finset.Nonempty.image h _)
   108‚Üí  else 0
   109‚Üí
   110‚Üí/-- Average distance of `G`. -/
   111‚Üínoncomputable def averageDistance (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñù :=
   112‚Üí  if Fintype.card Œ± > 1 then
   113‚Üí    (‚àë u ‚àà Finset.univ, ‚àë v ‚àà Finset.univ, (G.dist u v : ‚Ñù)) /
   114‚Üí      ((Fintype.card Œ± : ‚Ñù) * ((Fintype.card Œ± : ‚Ñù) - 1))
   115‚Üí  else
   116‚Üí    0
   117‚Üí
   118‚Üí/-- The floor of the average distance of `G`. -/
   119‚Üínoncomputable def path (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñï :=
   120‚Üí  if G.Connected then
   121‚Üí    Nat.floor (averageDistance G)
   122‚Üí  else
   123‚Üí    0
   124‚Üí
   125‚Üí/-- Auxiliary quantity `ecc` used in conjecture 34. -/
   126‚Üínoncomputable def ecc (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) (S : Set Œ±) : ‚Ñï :=
   127‚Üí  let s_comp := Finset.univ.filter (fun v => v ‚àâ S)
   128‚Üí  if h : s_comp.Nonempty then
   129‚Üí    (s_comp.image (fun v => distToSet G v S)).max' (Finset.Nonempty.image h _)
   130‚Üí  else 0
   131‚Üí
   132‚Üí/-- Average distance from all vertices to a given set. -/
   133‚Üínoncomputable def distavg (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) (S : Set Œ±) : ‚Ñù :=
   134‚Üí  if Fintype.card Œ± > 0 then
   135‚Üí    (‚àë v ‚àà Finset.univ, (distToSet G v S : ‚Ñù)) / (Fintype.card Œ± : ‚Ñù)
   136‚Üí  else
   137‚Üí    0
   138‚Üí
   139‚Üí/-- A family of paths covering all vertices without overlaps. -/
   140‚Üídef IsPathCover (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) (P : Finset (Finset Œ±)) : Prop :=
   141‚Üí  (‚àÄ s1 ‚àà P, ‚àÄ s2 ‚àà P, s1 ‚âÝ s2 ‚Üí Disjoint s1 s2) ‚àß
   142‚Üí  (Finset.univ ‚äÜ P.biUnion id) ‚àß
   143‚Üí  (‚àÄ s ‚àà P, ‚àÉ (u v : Œ±) (p : G.Walk u v), p.IsPath ‚àß s = p.support.toFinset)
   144‚Üí
   145‚Üí/-- Minimum size of a path cover of `G`. -/
   146‚Üínoncomputable def pathCoverNumber (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñï :=
   147‚Üí  sInf { k | ‚àÉ P : Finset (Finset Œ±), P.card = k ‚àß IsPathCover G P }
   148‚Üí
   149‚Üí/-- The same quantity as a real number. -/
   150‚Üínoncomputable def p (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñù := (pathCoverNumber G : ‚Ñù)
   151‚Üí
   152‚Üí/-- The Wiener index of `G`, which is the sum of distances between all
   153‚Üípairs of vertices. -/
   154‚Üínoncomputable def wienerIndex (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñï :=
   155‚Üí  ‚àë uv : Sym2 Œ±, uv.lift ‚ü®fun u v ‚Ü¶ G.dist u v, by simp [dist_comm]‚ü©
   156‚Üí
   157‚Üí/-- Auxiliary function for Szeged index: counts vertices closer to u than v. -/
   158‚Üínoncomputable def szeged_aux (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) (u v : Œ±) : ‚Ñï :=
   159‚Üí  (Finset.univ.filter (fun w => G.edist w u < G.edist w v)).card
   160‚Üí
   161‚Üí/-- The Szeged index of `G`.
   162‚Üí
   163‚ÜíThis is define as the sum `‚àë_{uv ‚àà E(G)} n_u(u,v) * n_v(u,v)` where
   164‚Üí`n_u(uv)` is the number of vertices closer to `u` than `v`.
   165‚Üí-/
   166‚Üínoncomputable def szegedIndex (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ñï :=
   167‚Üí  ‚àë e ‚àà G.edgeFinset,
   168‚Üí    e.lift ‚ü®fun u v => szeged_aux G u v * szeged_aux G v u, by simp [mul_comm]‚ü©
   169‚Üí
   170‚Üí/-- The average degree of `G`. -/
   171‚Üínoncomputable def averageDegree (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ñö  :=
   172‚Üí  (‚àë v, (G.degree v : ‚Ñö)) / (Fintype.card Œ± : ‚Ñö)
   173‚Üí
   174‚Üí/-- The multiset of degrees of a graph. -/
   175‚Üídef degreeMultiset (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : Multiset ‚Ñï :=
   176‚Üí  Finset.univ.val.map fun v => G.degree v
   177‚Üí
   178‚Üí/-- The annihilation number of a graph. This is the largest number of degrees that can be added
   179‚Üítogether without going over the total number of edges of that graph. -/
   180‚Üídef annihilationNumber (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ñï :=
   181‚Üí  -- Calculate the limit: The number of edges (Sum of degrees / 2)
   182‚Üí  letI limit := G.edgeFinset.card
   183‚Üí
   184‚Üí  -- The set of all multisets of degrees that sum to less than or equal to `limit`
   185‚Üí  Finset.Iic (degreeMultiset G)
   186‚Üí    |>.filter (fun S ‚Ü¶ Multiset.sum S ‚â§ limit)
   187‚Üí    |>.sup Multiset.card
   188‚Üí
   189‚Üí/--
   190‚ÜíComputes the annihilation number of a graph G.
   191‚Üí
   192‚ÜíIt calculates the degree sequence, sorts it ascendingly, and finds the largest prefix length 'k'
   193‚Üí(where `0 ‚â§ k ‚â§ |V(G)|`) such that the sum of the prefix is less than or equal to the sum of the corresponding suffix.
   194‚Üí-/
   195‚Üínoncomputable def annihilationNumber' (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ñï :=
   196‚Üí  -- 1. Get the degree sequence sorted in ascending order.
   197‚Üí  -- G.degree_list returns the list of degrees.
   198‚Üí  letI degrees := (Finset.univ.image fun v => G.degree v).sort (¬∑ ‚â§ ¬∑)
   199‚Üí
   200‚Üí  -- 2. Define the condition for the annihilation number.
   201‚Üí  -- k represents the number of smallest degrees considered (the length of the prefix).
   202‚Üí  letI condition (k : ‚Ñï) : Bool := (degrees.take k).sum ‚â§ (degrees.drop k).sum
   203‚Üí
   204‚Üí  -- 3. Find the maximum k in {0, ..., n} satisfying the condition.
   205‚Üí  -- List.range (n + 1) generates the list [0, 1, ..., n].
   206‚Üí  letI candidates := (List.range (Fintype.card Œ± + 1)).filter condition
   207‚Üí
   208‚Üí  -- 4. Return the maximum candidate.
   209‚Üí  -- The list of candidates is guaranteed to be non-empty because k=0 always satisfies
   210‚Üí  -- the condition (0 ‚â§ sum of all degrees).
   211‚Üí  candidates.getLast!
   212‚Üí
   213‚Üíset_option linter.unusedSectionVars false in
   214‚Üí-- TODO(Paul-Lez): debug the issue with the unused variable linter...
   215‚Üíproof_wanted annihilationNumberEq (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] :
   216‚Üí    annihilationNumber G = annihilationNumber' G
   217‚Üí
   218‚Üí/-!
   219‚Üí## Residue
   220‚ÜíThe residue of a graph is the number of zeros remaining after iteratively applying the Havel-Hakimi
   221‚Üíalgorithm to the degree sequence until all remaining degrees are zero.
   222‚Üí-/
   223‚Üí
   224‚Üí/--
   225‚ÜíHelper function: Performs one step of the Havel-Hakimi reduction on a degree sequence.
   226‚ÜíAssumes the input list `s` is sorted descending.
   227‚ÜíRemoves the first element `d`, decrements the next `d` elements by 1, and re-sorts the list descending.
   228‚Üí
   229‚ÜíNote: when `s` is the list of vertices arising from a simple graph, if the first index is `s` then
   230‚Üíthe degree list always has length at least `s+1` so this makes sense.
   231‚Üí-/
   232‚Üíprivate def havelHakimiStep (s : List ‚Ñï) : List ‚Ñï :=
   233‚Üí  match s with
   234‚Üí  | [] => []
   235‚Üí  | d :: rest =>
   236‚Üí    -- Split the rest into the part to decrement (first d elements) and the remaining part.
   237‚Üí    let (to_decrement, remaining) := rest.splitAt d
   238‚Üí    -- Decrement the elements
   239‚Üí    let decremented := to_decrement.map (¬∑ - 1)
   240‚Üí    -- Combine and re-sort descending.
   241‚Üí    (decremented ++ remaining).mergeSort (¬∑ ‚â• ¬∑)
   242‚Üí
   243‚Üí/--
   244‚ÜíAuxiliary function to calculate the residue recursively.
   245‚ÜíApplies Havel-Hakimi steps until the sequence consists only of zeros or is empty.
   246‚Üí-/
   247‚Üíprivate partial def residueAux : List ‚Ñï ‚Üí ‚Ñï
   248‚Üí  | [] => 0        -- Empty sequence, residue is 0.
   249‚Üí  | 0 :: s => 1 + s.length -- If the largest degree is 0 (and the list is sorted), all are 0.
   250‚Üí  | s => residueAux (havelHakimiStep s) -- Apply one reduction step and recurse.
   251‚Üí
   252‚Üí/--
   253‚ÜíComputes the residue of a graph G, ,i.e. the number of zeros remaining after iteratively applying the Havel-Hakimi
   254‚Üíalgorithm to the degree sequence until all remaining degrees are zero.
   255‚ÜíStarts with the descending degree sequence and applies the Havel-Hakimi process.
   256‚Üí-/
   257‚Üínoncomputable def residue (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ñï :=
   258‚Üí  -- Get the degree sequence sorted in descending order and apply `residueAux`.
   259‚Üí  residueAux ((Finset.univ.image fun v => G.degree v).sort (¬∑ ‚â• ¬∑))
   260‚Üí
   261‚Üí/--
   262‚ÜíFractional alpha. This is defined as
   263‚Üí$$
   264‚Üí\max_x \sum_{v \in V} x_v
   265‚Üí$$
   266‚Üísubject to $0 \le x_v \le 1$ for all $v \in V$ and
   267‚Üí$x_u + x_v \le 1$ whenever $(u, v)$ are connected by an edge.
   268‚Üí-/
   269‚Üínoncomputable def fractionalAlpha (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ñù :=
   270‚Üí  sSup {(‚àë i, x i) | (x : Œ± ‚Üí ‚Ñù) (_ : ‚àÄ v, x v ‚àà Set.Icc 0 1)
   271‚Üí    (_ : ‚àÄ u v, G.Adj u v ‚Üí x u + x v ‚â§ 1)}
   272‚Üí
   273‚Üí/--
   274‚ÜíLov√°sz Theta Function (œë(G))
   275‚ÜíThe Lov√°sz theta function is defined as:
   276‚Üíœë(G) = min Œª_max(A)
   277‚Üíwhere the minimum is taken over all symmetric matrices A such that:
   278‚Üí
   279‚ÜíA_ij = 1 for all i = j (diagonal entries are 1)
   280‚ÜíA_ij = 0 for all {i,j} ‚àà E (entries corresponding to edges are 0)
   281‚ÜíA is positive semidefinite
   282‚Üí
   283‚ÜíHere Œª_max(A) denotes the maximum eigenvalue of A.
   284‚Üí-/
   285‚Üínoncomputable def lovaczThetaFunction
   286‚Üí    (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ñù :=
   287‚Üí  sInf {(Matrix.IsHermitian.maxEigenvalue hA) | (A : Matrix Œ± Œ± ‚Ñù) (hA : A.IsHermitian)
   288‚Üí      (_ : ‚àÄ i, A i i = 1) (_ : ‚àÄ i j, G.Adj i j ‚Üí A i j = 0)}
   289‚Üí
   290‚Üí/--
   291‚ÜíGiven a simple graph `G` with adjacency matrix `A`, this is the number `n‚ÇÄ + min n‚Çä n‚Çã` where:
   292‚Üí- `n‚ÇÄ` is the multiplicity of zero as an eigenvalue of `A`
   293‚Üí- `n‚Çä` is the number of positive eigenvalues of `A` (counting multiplicities)
   294‚Üí- `n‚Çã` is the number of negative eigenvalues of `A` (counting multiplicities)
   295‚Üí-/
   296‚Üínoncomputable def cvetkovic (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ñï :=
   297‚Üí  letI A : Matrix Œ± Œ± ‚Ñù := G.adjMatrix ‚Ñù
   298‚Üí  letI spectrum : Multiset ‚Ñù := (Matrix.charpoly A).roots
   299‚Üí  letI positive_count : ‚Ñï := spectrum.countP (fun x => 0 < x)
   300‚Üí  letI negative_count : ‚Ñï := spectrum.countP (fun x => 0 > x)
   301‚Üí  letI zero_count : ‚Ñï := spectrum.countP (fun x => 0 = x)
   302‚Üí  zero_count + min positive_count negative_count
   303‚Üí

    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Acyclic
    18‚Üíimport Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Bipartite
    19‚Üíimport Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Matching
    20‚Üíimport Mathlib.Data.Real.Archimedean

    22‚Üí
    23‚Üínamespace SimpleGraph
    24‚Üí
    25‚Üíopen Classical Finset List
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üívariable {Œ± : Type*} [Fintype Œ±] [DecidableEq Œ±]
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üí/-- `Ls G` is the maximum number of leaves over all spanning trees of `G`.
    30‚ÜíIt is defined to be `0` when `G` is not connected. -/
    31‚Üínoncomputable def Ls (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ñù :=
    32‚Üí  letI spanningTrees := { T : Subgraph G | T.IsSpanning ‚àß IsTree T.coe }
    33‚Üí  letI leaves (T : Subgraph G) := T.verts.toFinset.filter (fun v => T.degree v = 1)
    34‚Üí  letI num_leaves (T : Subgraph G) := (leaves T).card
    35‚Üí  sSup (Set.image (fun T => (num_leaves T : ‚Ñù)) spanningTrees)
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí/-- `n G` is the number of vertices of `G` as a real number. -/
    38‚Üínoncomputable def n (_ : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñù := Fintype.card Œ±
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí/-- `m G` is the size of a maximum matching of `G`. -/
    41‚Üínoncomputable def m (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ñù :=
    42‚Üí  let matchings := { M : Subgraph G | M.IsMatching }
    43‚Üí  sSup (Set.image (fun M => (M.edgeSet.toFinset.card : ‚Ñù)) matchings)
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí/-- The maximum cardinality among all independent sets `s`
    46‚Üí    that maximize the quantity `|s| - |N(s)|`, where `N(s)`
    47‚Üí    is the neighborhood of the set `s`. -/
    48‚Üínoncomputable def aprime (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ñù :=
    49‚Üí  letI indep_sets : Finset (Finset Œ±) := univ.powerset.filter (fun s => G.IsIndepSet (s : Set Œ±))
    50‚Üí  letI diff (s : Finset Œ±) : ‚Ñ§ := (s.card : ‚Ñ§) - (‚ãÉ v ‚àà (s : Set Œ±), G.neighborSet v).toFinset.card
    51‚Üí  letI max_diff := (indep_sets.image diff).max
    52‚Üí  letI critical_sets := indep_sets.filter (fun s ‚Ü¶ diff s = max_diff.getD 0)
    53‚Üí  letI max_card := (critical_sets.image Finset.card).max
    54‚Üí  (max_card.getD 0 : ‚Ñù)
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí/-- `largestInducedForestSize G` is the size of a largest induced forest of `G`. -/
    57‚Üínoncomputable def largestInducedForestSize (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñï :=
    58‚Üí  sSup { n | ‚àÉ s : Finset Œ±, (G.induce s).IsAcyclic ‚àß s.card = n }
    59‚Üí
    60‚Üí/-- The degree sequence of a graph, sorted in nondecreasing order. -/
    61‚Üínoncomputable def degreeSequence (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : List ‚Ñï :=
    62‚Üí  (Finset.univ.val.map fun v : Œ± => G.degree v).sort (¬∑ ‚â§ ¬∑)
    63‚Üí
    64‚Üí/--
    65‚ÜíThe maximum number of occurrences of any term of the degree sequence of `G`.
    66‚Üí-/
    67‚Üínoncomputable def degreeSequenceMultiplicity (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ñï :=
    68‚Üí  letI degs := degreeSequence G
    69‚Üí  (List.max? (degs.map (fun d => degs.count d))).getD 0
    70‚Üí
    71‚Üí/-- `largestInducedBipartiteSubgraphSize G` is the size of a largest induced
    72‚Üíbipartite subgraph of `G`. -/
    73‚Üínoncomputable def largestInducedBipartiteSubgraphSize (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñï :=
    74‚Üí  sSup { n | ‚àÉ s : Finset Œ±, (G.induce s).IsBipartite ‚àß s.card = n }
    75‚Üí
    76‚Üí/-- `b G` is the number of vertices of a largest induced bipartite subgraph of `G`.
    77‚ÜíReturned as a real number. -/
    78‚Üínoncomputable def b (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñù :=
    79‚Üí  (largestInducedBipartiteSubgraphSize G : ‚Ñù)
    80‚Üí
    81‚Üí/-- Independence number of the neighbourhood of `v`. -/
    82‚Üínoncomputable def indepNeighborsCard (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) (v : Œ±) : ‚Ñï :=
    83‚Üí  (G.induce (G.neighborSet v)).indepNum
    84‚Üí
    85‚Üí/-- The same quantity as a real number. -/
    86‚Üínoncomputable def indepNeighbors (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) (v : Œ±) : ‚Ñù :=
    87‚Üí  (indepNeighborsCard G v : ‚Ñù)
    88‚Üí
    89‚Üí/-- Average of `indepNeighbors` over all vertices. -/
    90‚Üínoncomputable def averageIndepNeighbors (G : SimpleGraph Œ±) : ‚Ñù :=
    91‚Üí  (‚àë v ‚àà Finset.univ, indepNeighbors G v) / (Fintype.card Œ± : ‚Ñù)
    92‚Üí
    93‚Üí/-- A unit distance graph in ‚Ñù¬≤:
    94‚ÜíA graph where the vertices V are a collection of points in ‚Ñù¬≤ and there is
    95‚Üían edge between two points if and only if the distance between them is 1. -/
    96‚Üídef UnitDistancePlaneGraph (V : Set (EuclideanSpace ‚Ñù (Fin 2))) : SimpleGraph V where
    97‚Üí  Adj x y := Dist.dist x y = 1
    98‚Üí  symm x y := by simp [PseudoMetricSpace.dist_comm]
    99‚Üí
   100‚Üí/--
   101‚ÜíTwo walks are internally disjoint if they share no vertices other than their endpoints.
   102‚Üí-/
   103‚Üídef InternallyDisjoint {V : Type*} {G : SimpleGraph V} {u v x y : V}
   104‚Üí    (p : G.Walk u v) (q : G.Walk x y) : Prop :=
   105‚Üí  Disjoint p.support.tail.dropLast q.support.tail.dropLast
   106‚Üí
   107‚Üí/--
   108‚ÜíWe say a graph is infinitely connected if any two vertices are connected by infinitely many
   109‚Üípairwise disjoint paths.
   110‚Üí-/
   111‚Üídef InfinitelyConnected {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) : Prop :=
   112‚Üí  Pairwise fun u v ‚Ü¶ ‚àÉ P : Set (G.Walk u v),
   113‚Üí    P.Infinite ‚àß (‚àÄ p ‚àà P, p.IsPath) ‚àß P.Pairwise InternallyDisjoint
   114‚Üí
   115‚Üí/-- Infinite graphs: definitions for max degree and clique number so that the maximum
   116‚Üídegree (resp. clique number) of a graph with unbounded degree (resp. clique size) is
   117‚Üí`‚àû` rather than 0.
   118‚Üí-/
   119‚Üínoncomputable
   120‚Üídef edegree {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) (v : V) : ‚Ñï‚àû := (G.neighborSet v).encard
   121‚Üí
   122‚Üínoncomputable
   123‚Üídef emaxDegree {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) : ‚Ñï‚àû := ‚®Ü v, G.edegree v
   124‚Üí
   125‚Üínoncomputable
   126‚Üídef ecliqueNum {V : Type} (G : SimpleGraph V) : ‚Ñï‚àû := ‚®Ü (s : Finset V) (_ : G.IsClique s), #s
   127‚Üí
   128‚Üíend SimpleGraph

    20‚Üí/-!
    21‚Üí# D-Balanced graphs
    22‚Üí
    23‚Üí`SimpleGraph.IsBalanced G D` is the condition that the maximum degree of a simple graph $G$ is
    24‚Üíat most $D$ times the minimum degree.
    25‚Üí-/
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üíopen Fintype
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üínamespace SimpleGraph
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üívariable {V : Type*} [Fintype V]
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üí/--
    34‚ÜíWe call a graph $D$-balanced (or $D$-almost-regular) if the maximum degree is at most $D$ times the
    35‚Üíminimum degree.
    36‚Üí-/
    37‚Üídef IsBalanced (G : SimpleGraph V) (D : ‚Ñù) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : Prop :=
    38‚Üí    G.maxDegree <= D * G.minDegree
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üíend SimpleGraph

```lean
import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erd≈ës Problem 612

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/612](https://www.erdosproblems.com/612)

Let $G$ be a connected graph with $n$ vertices, minimum degree $d$, and diameter $D$.

The original conjecture of Erd≈ës, Pach, Pollack, and Tuza [EPPT89] stated:
- If $G$ contains no $K_{2r}$ and $(r-1)(3r+2) \mid d$ then
  $D \leq \frac{2(r-1)(3r+2)}{2r^2-1} \cdot \frac{n}{d} + O(1)$
- If $G$ contains no $K_{2r+1}$ and $(3r-1) \mid d$ then
  $D \leq \frac{3r-1}{r} \cdot \frac{n}{d} + O(1)$

The first case was disproven for $r \geq 2$ by Czabarka, Singgih, and Sz√©kely [CSS21].

The amended conjecture (due to [CSS21]) states that if $G$ contains no $K_{k+1}$ then
$D \leq (3 - 2/k) \cdot n/d + O(1)$.

This is known:
- For $k = 2$ ($K_3$-free), proved in [EPPT89]
- For $3$-colourable graphs (weaker than $K_4$-free), by Czabarka, Dankelmann,
  Sz√©kely [CDS09]
- For $4$-colourable graphs (weaker than $K_5$-free), by Czabarka, Smith,
  Sz√©kely [CSS23]

It is also known that any connected graph on $n$ vertices with minimum degree $d$ has
$D \leq 3n/(d+1) + O(1)$.

[EPPT89] Erd≈ës, P., Pach, J., Pollack, R., and Tuza, Zs., _Radius, diameter, and
minimum degree_. J. Combin. Theory Ser. B 47 (1989), 73-79.

[CSS21] Czabarka, √â., Singgih, O., and Sz√©kely, L.A., _Counterexample to a conjecture
of Erd≈ës, Pach, Pollack, and Tuza on diameter and minimum degree_. 2021.

[CDS09] Czabarka, √â., Dankelmann, P., and Sz√©kely, L.A., _Diameter and degree
conditions for $K_{t,t}$-free graphs_. 2009.

[CSS23] Czabarka, √â., Smith, S.J., and Sz√©kely, L.A., _Diameter bounds for
4-colourable graphs_. 2023.
-/

open SimpleGraph

namespace Erdos612

/--
**Erd≈ës Problem 612** (Amended conjecture, [CSS21]):

If $G$ is a connected $K_{k+1}$-free graph on $n$ vertices with minimum degree $d \geq 1$,
then
$$\operatorname{diam}(G) \leq \left(3 - \frac{2}{k}\right) \cdot \frac{n}{d} + C$$
for some constant $C$ depending only on $k$.
-/
@[category research open, AMS 5]
theorem erdos_612 (k : ‚Ñï) (hk : k ‚â• 2) :
    ‚àÉ C : ‚Ñù, ‚àÄ (n : ‚Ñï) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) [DecidableRel G.Adj],
      G.Connected ‚Üí
      G.CliqueFree (k + 1) ‚Üí
      G.minDegree ‚â• 1 ‚Üí
      (G.diam : ‚Ñù) ‚â§ (3 - 2 / (k : ‚Ñù)) * ((n : ‚Ñù) / (G.minDegree : ‚Ñù)) + C := by
  sorry

/--
**Erd≈ës Problem 612** (Known case $k = 2$, [EPPT89]):

If $G$ is a connected triangle-free graph on $n$ vertices with minimum degree $d \geq 1$,
then
$$\operatorname{diam}(G) \leq 2 \cdot \frac{n}{d} + C$$
for some absolute constant $C$.
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 5]
theorem erdos_612.variants.triangle_free :
    ‚àÉ C : ‚Ñù, ‚àÄ (n : ‚Ñï) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) [DecidableRel G.Adj],
      G.Connected ‚Üí
      G.CliqueFree 3 ‚Üí
      G.minDegree ‚â• 1 ‚Üí
      (G.diam : ‚Ñù) ‚â§ 2 * ((n : ‚Ñù) / (G.minDegree : ‚Ñù)) + C := by
  sorry

/--
Known general bound ([EPPT89]):

Any connected graph on $n$ vertices with minimum degree $d \geq 1$ has
$$\operatorname{diam}(G) \leq \frac{3n}{d+1} + O(1).$$
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 5]
theorem erdos_612.variants.general_bound :
    ‚àÉ C : ‚Ñù, ‚àÄ (n : ‚Ñï) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) [DecidableRel G.Adj],
      G.Connected ‚Üí
      G.minDegree ‚â• 1 ‚Üí
      (G.diam : ‚Ñù) ‚â§ 3 * ((n : ‚Ñù) / ((G.minDegree : ‚Ñù) + 1)) + C := by
  sorry

end Erdos612
```
